```{r}
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("EMOS" = "#2ca02c",
                                "QR" = "#ff7f0e",
                                "RF" = "#1f77b4")) +
    labs(title = "Seasonal CRPS Performance",
         x = "Season", y = "Mean CRPS") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  
  print(p1)
  
  # Plot 2: Improvement percentages
  p2 <- ggplot(data_plot,
               aes(x = season, y = crps_improvement_pct, fill = method)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", crps_improvement_pct)),
              position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
              vjust = -0.5, size = 3) +
    facet_wrap(~city) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("EMOS" = "#2ca02c",
                                "QR" = "#ff7f0e",
                                "RF" = "#1f77b4")) +
    labs(title = "Seasonal Improvement over Raw Ensemble",
         x = "Season", y = "CRPS Improvement (%)") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  
  print(p2)
}

# ============================================
# EXECUTE ANALYSIS
# ============================================

# Run the seasonal analysis
seasonal_analysis <- analyze_seasonal_performance(
  results = results_vs_wow,  # Your post-processing results
  data_amsterdam = data_amsterdam,
  data_rotterdam = data_rotterdam,
  lead_time = 24
)

# Create visualizations
plot_seasonal_analysis(seasonal_analysis)

# Save results
write.csv(seasonal_analysis$seasonal_comparison_full,
          here("results", "some_results", "seasonal_crps_full.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

write.csv(seasonal_analysis$seasonal_avg,
          here("results", "some_results", "seasonal_summary.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

cat("\n✓ Seasonal analysis complete!\n")

```

#PEV framework
```{r}
# ============================================
# PEV ANALYSIS FOR WBGT POST-PROCESSING
# ============================================

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Main PEV calculation function
calculate_pev <- function(obs, pred_mean, pred_sd, threshold, cost_loss_ratio) {
  
  # Remove N
  valid_idx <- !is.na(obs) & !is.na(pred_mean) & !is.na(pred_sd)
  obs <- obs[valid_idx]
  pred_mean <- pred_mean[valid_idx]
  pred_sd <- pred_sd[valid_idx]
  n <- length(obs)
  
  if(n < 10) return(NA)
  
  # Calculate forecast probability of exceeding threshold
  prob_exceed <- 1 - pnorm(threshold, pred_mean, pred_sd)
  
  # Binary decision based on cost-loss ratio
  take_action <- prob_exceed > cost_loss_ratio
  
  # Observed event
  event_occurred <- obs > threshold
  
  # Contingency table elements
  hits <- sum(take_action & event_occurred)
  false_alarms <- sum(take_action & !event_occurred)
  misses <- sum(!take_action & event_occurred)
  correct_negatives <- sum(!take_action & !event_occurred)
  
  # Observed relative frequency
  orf <- sum(event_occurred) / n
  
  # Handle edge cases
  if(orf == 0 || orf == 1) return(0)
  
  # Calculate expenses
  expense_forecast <- (hits + false_alarms) * cost_loss_ratio + misses * 1
  
  # Climatology strategy expense
  if(cost_loss_ratio < orf) {
    # Always protect
    expense_clim <- n * cost_loss_ratio
  } else {
    # Never protect
    expense_clim <- sum(event_occurred) * 1
  }
  
  # Perfect forecast expense
  expense_perfect <- sum(event_occurred) * cost_loss_ratio
  
  # PEV calculation
  if(abs(expense_clim - expense_perfect) < 1e-10) {
    pev <- 0
  } else {
    pev <- (expense_clim - expense_forecast) / (expense_clim - expense_perfect)
  }
  
  return(pev)
}

# Calculate PEV for raw ensemble
calculate_pev_raw <- function(data, threshold, cost_loss_ratio, lead_time = 6) {
  
  # Filter data
  data_filtered <- data %>%
    filter(lead_time_hrs == lead_time)
  
  # Aggregate ensemble
  data_agg <- data_filtered %>%
    group_by(Valid_datetime) %>%
    summarise(
      wow_max = first(wow_wbgt_max),
      ens_mean = mean(WBGT_C, na.rm = TRUE),
      ens_sd = sd(WBGT_C, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = 'drop'
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(wow_max))
  
  # Calculate PEV
  pev <- calculate_pev(
    obs = data_agg$wow_max,
    pred_mean = data_agg$ens_mean,
    pred_sd = data_agg$ens_sd,
    threshold = threshold,
    cost_loss_ratio = cost_loss_ratio
  )
  
  return(pev)
}

# Calculate PEV curves for all methods
calculate_pev_curves <- function(results, data_amsterdam, data_rotterdam,
                                thresholds = c(20, 25, 30),
                                cl_ratios = seq(0.01, 0.5, by = 0.02)) {
  
  all_pev_results <- list()
  
  for(city in unique(results$city)) {
    for(method in unique(results$method)) {
      for(threshold in thresholds) {
        
        # Filter results
        method_data <- results %>%
          filter(city == !!city, method == !!method)
        
        if(nrow(method_data) < 100) next
        
        # Check if threshold is reasonable
        if(threshold > quantile(method_data$wow_max, 0.99, na.rm = TRUE)) next
        
        # Calculate PEV for each cost-loss ratio
        pev_values <- sapply(cl_ratios, function(cl) {
          calculate_pev(
            obs = method_data$wow_max,
            pred_mean = method_data$pred_mean,
            pred_sd = method_data$pred_sd,
            threshold = threshold,
            cost_loss_ratio = cl
          )
        })
        
        # Store results
        all_pev_results[[length(all_pev_results) + 1]] <- data.frame(
          city = city,
          method = method,
          threshold = threshold,
          cost_loss_ratio = cl_ratios,
          pev = pev_values,
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
      }
    }
    
    # Add raw ensemble results
    raw_data <- if(city == "Amsterdam") data_amsterdam else data_rotterdam
    
    for(threshold in thresholds) {
      pev_values_raw <- sapply(cl_ratios, function(cl) {
        calculate_pev_raw(raw_data, threshold, cl, lead_time = 6)
      })
      
      all_pev_results[[length(all_pev_results) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        city = city,
        method = "RAW",
        threshold = threshold,
        cost_loss_ratio = cl_ratios,
        pev = pev_values_raw,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
  }
  
  return(bind_rows(all_pev_results))
}

# Calculate integrated PEV (area under curve)
calculate_integrated_pev <- function(pev_curves) {
  
  integrated <- pev_curves %>%
    group_by(city, method, threshold) %>%
    summarise(
      integrated_pev = {
        valid_idx <- !is.na(pev) & pev > -0.5  # Remove extreme negative values
        if(sum(valid_idx) < 2) {
          NA
        } else {
          x <- cost_loss_ratio[valid_idx]
          y <- pev[valid_idx]
          # Trapezoidal integration
          sum(diff(x) * (y[-1] + y[-length(y)]) / 2)
        }
      },
      max_pev = max(pev[!is.na(pev)], na.rm = TRUE),
      optimal_cl = cost_loss_ratio[which.max(pev)],
      positive_range = sum(pev > 0, na.rm = TRUE) / length(pev),
      .groups = 'drop'
    )
  
  return(integrated)
}

# Plot PEV curves
plot_pev_curves <- function(pev_curves, selected_threshold = 25) {
  
  # Filter data
  plot_data <- pev_curves %>%
    filter(threshold == selected_threshold,
           !is.na(pev),
           pev > -1)  # Remove extreme negative values for visualization
  
  # Define colors
  method_colors <- c("RAW" = "gray60",
                    "EMOS" = "#2ca02c",
                    "QR" = "#ff7f0e",
                    "RF" = "#1f77b4")
  
  # Create plot
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = cost_loss_ratio, y = pev, 
                             color = method, linetype = method)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.3) +
    geom_line(size = 1.2) +
    facet_wrap(~city, ncol = 2) +
    scale_color_manual(values = method_colors) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.2, 1)) +
    labs(
      title = sprintf("Potential Economic Value Curves (Threshold = %d°C)", selected_threshold),
      subtitle = "Higher values indicate better economic value for decision-making",
      x = "Cost/Loss Ratio",
      y = "Potential Economic Value",
      color = "Method",
      linetype = "Method"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
  
  return(p)
}

# Create comparison table
create_pev_comparison_table <- function(integrated_pev) {
  
  # Reshape for comparison
  comparison <- integrated_pev %>%
    select(city, method, threshold, integrated_pev, max_pev, optimal_cl) %>%
    arrange(city, threshold, desc(integrated_pev)) %>%
    mutate(
      integrated_pev = round(integrated_pev, 3),
      max_pev = round(max_pev, 3),
      optimal_cl = round(optimal_cl, 3)
    )
  
  # Calculate improvement over RAW
  improvement <- comparison %>%
    group_by(city, threshold) %>%
    mutate(
      raw_integrated = integrated_pev[method == "RAW"],
      improvement_pct = round((integrated_pev - raw_integrated) / abs(raw_integrated) * 100, 1)
    ) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    select(-raw_integrated)
  
  return(improvement)
}

# Generate LaTeX table
create_pev_latex_table <- function(pev_comparison) {
  
  cat("\n=== LATEX TABLE FOR PEV RESULTS ===\n")
  cat("\\begin{table}[h!]\n")
  cat("\\centering\n")
  cat("\\caption{Integrated PEV and maximum PEV values for different thresholds}\n")
  cat("\\label{tab:pev_analysis}\n")
  cat("\\begin{tabular}{llccccc}\n")
  cat("\\hline\n")
  cat("City & Method & Threshold & Integrated PEV & Max PEV & Optimal C/L & Improvement (\\%) \\\\\n")
  cat("\\hline\n")
  
  for(city_name in unique(pev_comparison$city)) {
    city_data <- pev_comparison %>% filter(city == city_name)
    
    first_row <- TRUE
    for(i in 1:nrow(city_data)) {
      if(first_row) {
        cat(sprintf("\\multirow{%d}{*}{%s}\n", nrow(city_data), city_name))
        first_row <- FALSE
      }
      
      improvement_str <- ifelse(city_data$method[i] == "RAW", 
                               "--", 
                               sprintf("%.1f", city_data$improvement_pct[i]))
      
      cat(sprintf(" & %s & %d & %.3f & %.3f & %.3f & %s \\\\\n",
                 city_data$method[i],
                 city_data$threshold[i],
                 city_data$integrated_pev[i],
                 city_data$max_pev[i],
                 city_data$optimal_cl[i],
                 improvement_str))
    }
    
    if(city_name == unique(pev_comparison$city)[1]) {
      cat("\\hline\n")
    }
  }
  
  cat("\\hline\n")
  cat("\\end{tabular}\n")
  cat("\\end{table}\n")
}

# ============================================
# MAIN PEV ANALYSIS EXECUTION
# ============================================

run_pev_analysis <- function(results, data_amsterdam, data_rotterdam) {
  
  cat("\n========================================\n")
  cat("   POTENTIAL ECONOMIC VALUE ANALYSIS    \n")
  cat("========================================\n\n")
  
  # Define thresholds based on data range
  # Check actual data range
  wow_range <- range(results$wow_max, na.rm = TRUE)
  cat(sprintf("WOW max range: %.1f to %.1f°C\n", wow_range[1], wow_range[2]))
  
  # Set meaningful thresholds
  thresholds <- c(20, 25)  # Focus on heat stress thresholds
  
  # Calculate PEV curves
  cat("\nCalculating PEV curves...\n")
  pev_curves <- calculate_pev_curves(
    results = results,
    data_amsterdam = data_amsterdam,
    data_rotterdam = data_rotterdam,
    thresholds = thresholds,
    cl_ratios = seq(0.01, 0.5, by = 0.02)
  )
  
  # Calculate integrated PEV
  cat("Calculating integrated PEV...\n")
  integrated_pev <- calculate_integrated_pev(pev_curves)
  
  # Create comparison table
  pev_comparison <- create_pev_comparison_table(integrated_pev)
  
  # Print summary
  cat("\n=== INTEGRATED PEV SUMMARY ===\n")
  print(pev_comparison)
  
  # Create plots
  cat("\nGenerating PEV curves...\n")
  
  # Plot for each threshold
  plots <- list()
  for(thresh in thresholds) {
    plots[[as.character(thresh)]] <- plot_pev_curves(pev_curves, thresh)
    print(plots[[as.character(thresh)]])
  }
  
  # Generate LaTeX table
  create_pev_latex_table(pev_comparison %>% filter(threshold == 25))
  
  # Save results
  write.csv(pev_curves, here("results", "some_results", "pev_curves_full.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  write.csv(pev_comparison, here("results", "some_results", "pev_comparison_table.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  
  cat("\n✓ PEV analysis complete!\n")
  
  return(list(
    curves = pev_curves,
    integrated = integrated_pev,
    comparison = pev_comparison,
    plots = plots
  ))
}

# Execute PEV analysis
pev_results <- run_pev_analysis(results_vs_wow, data_amsterdam, data_rotterdam)

# Additional analysis: Best method by cost-loss ratio range
analyze_pev_by_cl_range <- function(pev_curves) {
  
  # Define C/L ranges relevant for different users
  cl_ranges <- list(
    "Risk-averse (0.01-0.10)" = c(0.01, 0.10),
    "Moderate (0.10-0.25)" = c(0.10, 0.25),
    "Risk-tolerant (0.25-0.50)" = c(0.25, 0.50)
  )
  
  range_analysis <- list()
  
  for(range_name in names(cl_ranges)) {
    range_limits <- cl_ranges[[range_name]]
    
    range_pev <- pev_curves %>%
      filter(cost_loss_ratio >= range_limits[1],
             cost_loss_ratio <= range_limits[2]) %>%
      group_by(city, method, threshold) %>%
      summarise(
        mean_pev = mean(pev, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = 'drop'
      ) %>%
      group_by(city, threshold) %>%
      slice_max(mean_pev, n = 1) %>%
      mutate(cl_range = range_name)
    
    range_analysis[[range_name]] <- range_pev
  }
  
  final_analysis <- bind_rows(range_analysis)
  
  cat("\n=== BEST METHOD BY COST-LOSS RATIO RANGE ===\n")
  print(final_analysis)
  
  return(final_analysis)
}

# Run additional analysis
cl_range_analysis <- analyze_pev_by_cl_range(pev_results$curves)
```
